class Solution {
public:
    int countGoodStrings(int low, int high, int zero, int one) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 +7;
        vector<long long> dp(high + 1, 0);
        dp[0] = 1;

            dp[i] = a % MOD;
        }
        long long total = 0;
        for (int i=low; i<=high; ++i) {
            total = (total + dp[i]) % MOD;
        }
        return total;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int countGoodStrings(int low, int high, int zero, int one) {
        const int MOD = 1000000007;
        vector<long long> dp(high + 1, 0);
        dp[0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= high; ++i) {
            long long count = 0;
            if (i >= zero) count += dp[i - zero];
            if (i >= one) count += dp[i - one];
            dp[i] = count % MOD;
        }
        long long res = 0;
        for (int i = low; i <= high; ++i) {
            res = (res + dp[i]) % MOD;
        }
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(high)
space complexity: O(high)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses dynamic programming to count valid string constructions. dp[i] stores the number of ways to form a string of length i. For each length i, it considers adding either a '0' block or a '1' block. The solution sums valid counts between [low, high]. Strengths: Efficient O(high) time and space. Weaknesses: High memory usage for very large high values.
